message:
fuzz: add robot_envelope_json target with structure-aware arbitrary harness and matching Cargo.lock resolve

Adds `fuzz/fuzz_targets/robot_envelope_json.rs` -- a
structure-aware libfuzzer harness for `RobotEnvelope` JSON
round-trip plus mistyped-field / missing-field / extra-field
decode modes. The harness's `[[bin]]` stanza (name, path) was
already committed to `fuzz/Cargo.toml` at lines 90-91; the source
file and the matching resolver catch-up in `Cargo.lock` land
here.

Why structure-aware instead of raw bytes
----------------------------------------
A plain `fuzz_target!(|data: &[u8]|)` spends nearly all its
budget producing JSON that does not parse, so it exercises the
tokenizer but never reaches the envelope-validation logic. The
harness uses `arbitrary::Arbitrary` to drive a `RawEnvelopeDocument`
whose shape mirrors the on-wire JSON (timestamp, source, data,
degraded), plus a `DecodeMode` enum that selects one of:

  * `Valid` -- emit a well-formed envelope; oracle: must decode
    and round-trip byte-for-byte.
  * `Missing(field)` -- omit one of the required fields (timestamp,
    source, data); oracle: must return a typed decode error, must
    not panic.
  * `Mistyped(field)` -- emit the correct key with a wrong-type
    value (string where number expected, etc.); oracle: must
    return a typed decode error, must not panic.

`extra_fields` is populated on every mode to exercise the
"unknown fields" path on both valid and invalid envelopes --
unknown fields in valid envelopes must be ignored; unknown fields
alongside an invalid structure must not mask the underlying
error.

Caps
----
`MAX_STR_LEN = 96` and `MAX_ITEMS = 6` bound per-field allocation
so the harness keeps a high exec/s rate. The derived
`Arbitrary` impl produces deterministic instances from the raw
fuzzer input, so minimized crashes are stable.

Lockfile
--------
`Cargo.lock` picks up:

  * `frankenterm-fuzz` now links `arbitrary`, `frankenterm-escape-parser`,
    `leb128`, `serde_json`, `serde_yaml` -- all of which had already
    been declared in `fuzz/Cargo.toml` in prior commits (4795a57f,
    4e8f8dc4, 46986fd1, 70cd4215) but without a Cargo.lock re-resolve.
    This harness is the one that links `serde_json` for round-trip
    validation, so the resolver now pulls it into the fuzz crate.
  * `base91` gains `byteorder` -- unrelated upstream pull, but
    shows up in the resolve because base91 is in the same workspace.
  * `zerocopy` dep-tree picks up `tracing` -- same, unrelated
    transitive bump.

No top-level `[dependencies]` changes in this commit -- every new
dep in the lockfile is either already declared in a `Cargo.toml`
upstream or is a pure transitive catch-up.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1261,6 +1261,7 @@ dependencies = [
  "anyhow",
  "asupersync",
  "base91",
+ "byteorder",
  "config",
  "frankenterm-term",
  "leb128",
@@ -3386,12 +3387,18 @@ dependencies = [
 name = "frankenterm-fuzz"
 version = "0.0.0"
 dependencies = [
+ "arbitrary",
  "codec",
  "frankenterm-core",
+ "frankenterm-escape-parser",
+ "leb128",
  "libfuzzer-sys",
  "rusqlite",
  "serde",
+ "serde_json",
+ "serde_yaml",
  "toml 0.8.23",
+ "tru",
 ]
 
 [[package]]
@@ -6587,6 +6594,7 @@ dependencies = [
  "termwiz 0.24.0",
  "textwrap",
  "thiserror 2.0.18",
+ "tracing",
  "url",
  "winapi",
 ]
